sections_cited: section 21 of the Sales Tax Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Suit No.186 of 2024 Suit No.385 of 2024 Suit No.398 of 2024 Suit No.384 of 2024 ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge(s) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection at flag ‘A’. 2. For hearing of CMA No.4519/2024. 3. For hearing of CMA No.2863/2024. 10.09.2024 Mr. Ahmed Masood, advocate for the plaintiff. Mr. Shahid Ali Qureshi, advocate for the defendant. Barrister Syed Ahsan Ali Shah, advocate for the defendant. Mr. Ameeruddin, advocate for the defendant. Ms. Alizeh Bashir, Assistant Attorney General. It is jointly stated that the issue before the court is with respect to suspension of sales tax registration. Prima facie, the orders are assailable per section 21 of the Sales Tax Act 1990. It is submitted that the plaintiffs shall seek recourse before the statutory hierarchy and the defendants will not take any further coercive action against the plaintiffs pending adjudication of the representation; to be filed within 15 days hereof. It is submitted that these suits be disposed off as aforesaid. Order accordingly. Office to place copy hereof in each connected file. Judge Khuhro/PA
